metadata.task_description: TDD: Settings Persistence Fix - Fix AGENT API configuration and other settings not persisting after page refresh

project_context.description: Novel Agent - An AI-powered multi-agent collaborative novel writing system with knowledge base, real-time WebSocket communication, and comprehensive project management capabilities.

layers: Presentation Layer (FastAPI web interface with WebSocket support)
Business Logic Layer (Multi-agent coordination and novel generation workflows)
Data Access Layer (JSON file storage with knowledge base and project management)

key_components.name: Web Application Server
key_components.description: FastAPI application with RESTful API endpoints, WebSocket support for real-time updates, static file serving, and Jinja2 templating. Manages API settings, project CRUD operations, and agent coordination.

key_components.name: Multi-Agent System
key_components.description: Eight specialized agents (Worldbuilder, Outliner, ChapterWriter, Polisher, Evaluator, ContinuousWriter, Communicator, MessageBus) implementing the Coordinator-Worker pattern for collaborative novel generation.

key_components.name: Configuration Management
key_components.description: Pydantic-based configuration system with LLM defaults, API endpoints, writing configs, and server settings. Manages agent behaviors and API integration.

key_components.name: Data Persistence Layer
key_components.description: JSON-based storage system for agent configurations, global API settings, projects metadata, and trend statistics. Currently affected by settings persistence bug.

key_components.name: Knowledge Base System
key_components.description: Three-layer architecture (data/logic/application) with hybrid vector+keyword search, semantic chunking, and chapter management using ChromaDB.

key_components.name: Context Management
key_components.description: Manages character profiles, world-building settings, and narrative context throughout the writing process.

key_components.name: Utility Modules
key_components.description: Supporting utilities including caching, dashboard metrics, export functionality, logging, MCP management, retry logic, token statistics, and validators.

key_components.name: Project Manager
key_components.description: Handles project lifecycle operations including creation, loading, saving, and deletion of novel writing projects.

error_handling.pattern: Mixed - some endpoints use try-except, others raise HTTPException
anti_patterns: Inconsistent error response formats (JSONResponse vs HTTPException)

testing.patterns: Fixtures for mocks, async test support, MagicMock for mocking

source_code.description: FastAPI application with settings save/load endpoints. Contains POST /api/settings (lines 289-343) that writes .env file but doesn't reload config. Critical bug location.

key_sections.get_settings: lines 277-287 - Returns current config (stale after refresh)
key_sections.save_settings: lines 289-343 - Writes .env file, updates runtime config, recreates coordinator
key_sections.save_global_api_config: lines 610-637 - Saves JSON config without reload

issues: No error handling on .env file write (line 325)
Updates runtime config after file write (wrong order)
No config reload mechanism after .env changes

source_code.description: Global configuration module with load_dotenv() at line 20 (called once at import). LLMConfig class (lines 23-29) captures os.getenv() values at class definition time. Global config singleton at line 84. ROOT CAUSE of persistence bug.

key_sections.load_dotenv: line 20 - Only runs once at module import
key_sections.LLMConfig: lines 23-29 - Defaults captured at class definition

issues: os.getenv() values cached at class definition

source_code.description: AgentConfigManager with separate JSON-based config persistence. Has _load_configs() (lines 192-213), _load_global_config() (lines 215-240), _save_configs() (lines 273-282), _save_global_config() (lines 284-294). No reload mechanism after initialization.

key_sections._load_configs: lines 192-213 - Loads agent configs from JSON
key_sections._save_configs: lines 273-282 - Saves to JSON (no error handling)
key_sections._save_global_config: lines 284-294 - Saves global config (no error handling)
key_sections.set_global_config: lines 305-329 - Updates config but no reload

issues: No error handling on file write operations
Dual config system with .env (config.py) operates independently

source_code.description: Frontend settings module with loadGlobalAPISettings() function. Fetches config from /api/api-configs endpoint but doesn't trigger config reload after save.

key_sections.loadGlobalAPISettings: Loads current settings on tab switch
key_sections.save_handlers: POST to /api/settings but no reload trigger

issues: May display stale values after page refresh

source_code.description: Main application entry point with DOMContentLoaded listener at line 5001. May need to trigger config reload after settings save.

key_sections.module_switch: May need reload on settings page access

source_code.description: Application entry point calling load_dotenv() at lines 20-21 before importing config module. Demonstrates import order and early dotenv loading.
issues: Shows load_dotenv() only runs once at startup

source_code.description: Base agent class using config.llm values at lines 105-106. Shows runtime config consumption pattern and dependency on stale config values.
issues: Depends on stale config values after refresh

config.description: Environment variables file for API configuration. Updated by POST /api/settings but config object not reloaded after changes.
Python dependencies including python-dotenv>=1.0.0, fastapi>=0.100.0, pydantic>=2.0.0, pytest.
JSON storage for global API configuration. Written by agent_config.py but no reload mechanism.
Agent-specific configuration storage. Written by agent_config.py with no error handling.

tests.description: Workflow integration tests with pytest, fixtures for mocks, async test support. Good reference for TDD patterns.
Agent tests showing config usage patterns.

internal.usage: Uses global config object for settings API endpoints
Uses config for knowledge base initialization

external.usage: Loads .env file into os.environ. load_dotenv() called once at module import.
external.critical_issue: Only runs once at import, no reload mechanism exists

external.usage: BaseModel for config validation. Default values evaluated at class definition.
external.critical_issue: Immutable by default, requires re-instantiation for value updates
external.fix_required: Add Config.reload() method to re-instantiate config objects

external.usage: Web framework for API endpoints. HTTPException for errors, JSONResponse for responses.
external.fix_required: Standardize to JSONResponse with success/error fields

external.usage: Testing framework with fixtures, async support, MagicMock for mocking
external.usage_pattern: Fixtures for mocks, async test support

external.usage: Path operations for file I/O. Path.write_text() raises OSError on failures
external.critical_issue: No error handling on write operations
external.fix_required: Wrap writes in try-except with atomic write pattern

breaking_changes: Config reload mechanism may affect all config-dependent modules
Error handling changes may break frontend expecting different response format
Hot-reload may require coordinator recreation (already implemented in app.py:334-341)

conflict_detection.mitigation_strategy: Incremental TDD approach: (1) Add tests for current behavior, (2) Implement Config.reload() method, (3) Add error handling to file writes, (4) Create /api/settings/reload endpoint, (5) Update frontend to call reload after save. Maintain backward compatibility by making reload optional (save-for-restart vs hot-reload).

summary.key_patterns: Configuration Load Pattern: Module import time load_dotenv() → os.getenv() cached in LLMConfig defaults → Runtime mutations don't persist → Page refresh re-loads unchanged defaults. Save Pattern: POST endpoint writes .env but config object remains stale.
summary.integration_points: POST /api/settings (app.py:290) - saves to .env but doesn't trigger config reload → GET /api/settings (app.py:277) - returns stale config object → LLMConfig.__init__ (config.py:23-29) - only reads os.getenv once at module load → Frontend loadGlobalAPISettings() - displays config without knowing it's stale. Critical gap: No bridge between .env write and config object reload.

summary.key_patterns: Module-level os.getenv() calls create frozen state. Runtime config values are captured at module import time via os.getenv() calls. When .env file is updated and page refreshes, the Python process still has the old values because load_dotenv() was already called at module import and os.getenv() caches values at first call.
summary.integration_points: Config Initialization (novel_agent/config.py:20) - load_dotenv() called at module import, only executed once. Web App Startup (novel_agent/web/app.py:47-70) - lifespan() calls config.init() at line 50, no config reload. Settings Save Endpoint (novel_agent/web/app.py:289-343) - POST saves to .env (line 325) and updates runtime config (lines 328-331), but os.getenv() still returns old cached values.

summary.key_patterns: Silent Failures: Lines 1204, 1308, 1844 use 'except Exception: pass'. No Write Error Handling: Lines 325, 1226, 279-280, 291-293 have no try-except. No Transaction Safety: Multiple files updated sequentially without atomicity. Inconsistent Error Responses: Some endpoints return JSONResponse with success:false, others raise HTTPException.
summary.integration_points: Critical Write Operations (No Error Handling): novel_agent/web/app.py:325 - save_settings writes .env file (CRITICAL). novel_agent/web/app.py:1226 - save_knowledge_base_config writes JSON (CRITICAL). novel_agent/agent_config.py:279 - save agent configs (HIGH). novel_agent/agent_config.py:291 - save global API config (HIGH).

key_issues: No error handling on .env file write (line 325)
Updates runtime config after file write (wrong order)
No config reload mechanism after .env changes
load_dotenv() only runs once at module import
os.getenv() values cached at class definition time
No error handling on file write operations

conflict_indicators.description: Dual config systems (.env and JSON) operate independently with no synchronization
File write operations without error handling violate data integrity requirements
No bridge between .env file write and config object reload

clarification_needs.question: Should settings persistence include hot-reload (config takes effect immediately without server restart) or is save-for-next-start acceptable?
clarification_needs.context: Current implementation writes .env but config object stays stale. Hot-reload requires either (a) calling dotenv_values(override=True) to refresh os.environ, or (b) adding config.reload() method and calling from POST endpoint.
options: Hot-reload: Settings take effect immediately, reload config object after POST /api/save
Save-for-restart: Settings persist to .env but require server restart to take effect (minimal code change)
Hybrid: API config hot-reloads immediately, other settings require restart (selective reload)

clarification_needs.question: Should the config reload happen automatically on every page refresh, or only when settings are explicitly saved?
clarification_needs.context: Current code saves settings to .env file and updates runtime config object, but page refresh loses changes because os.getenv() caches old values.
options: Auto-reload config on every page load/refresh (call reload endpoint from frontend on module switch)
Only reload after explicit save action, show success message after reload completes
Hybrid: Auto-reload on settings page access, lazy load elsewhere
Restart server process on settings change (requires process manager)

clarification_needs.question: Should settings write failures prevent runtime config updates?
clarification_needs.context: Current code updates runtime config even if file write might fail (lines 328-341 in app.py). This creates inconsistency between memory and disk state.
options: Validate write success first, then update runtime (with rollback if write fails)
Update runtime optimistically, log file write errors, notify user of partial save
Use atomic write pattern (temp file + atomic rename) to guarantee all-or-nothing
Add retry mechanism with exponential backoff for transient file write failures

clarification_needs.question: Should multi-file updates be atomic?
clarification_needs.context: Endpoints like save_knowledge_base_config write both JSON config and .env file (lines 1226, 1266). If process crashes between writes, configs become inconsistent.
options: Add atomic write pattern using temp files + atomic rename
Add rollback mechanism to restore previous state on error
Accept eventual consistency, add config repair endpoint
Merge configs into single file to avoid multi-file transactions

constraints.constraint: load_dotenv() is one-time at import, cannot reload without restarting process or using dotenv_values() override=True
Pydantic BaseModel defaults are set at class definition, not per-instance. Cannot modify BaseModel instances after creation. Must re-instantiate to update values
No Transaction System: Cannot wrap multiple file writes in atomic transaction
Python Process Lifecycle: Config loaded once at startup, persists for process lifetime. Page refresh doesn't restart Python process
Module Import System: Python modules imported once per process (sys.modules cache). Global singleton pattern (config.py:84) prevents re-import

all_patterns.patterns: Configuration Load Pattern: Module import time load_dotenv() → os.getenv() cached in LLMConfig defaults → Runtime mutations don't persist → Page refresh re-loads unchanged defaults

Save Pattern: POST endpoint writes .env but config object remains stale. Fix Pattern: Need hot-reload mechanism - either reload_dotenv() after save or add config.reload() method called by POST endpoint

Module-level os.getenv() calls create frozen state at class definition time. Fix Pattern Required: Option 1: Config reload method. Option 2: Dynamic property access. Option 3: Config manager with reload endpoint

Silent Failures Anti-pattern: 'except Exception: pass' swallows errors. No Write Error Handling: File writes without try-except. No Transaction Safety: Multiple files updated sequentially without atomicity

all_integration_points.points: POST /api/settings (app.py:290) - saves to .env but doesn't trigger config reload → GET /api/settings (app.py:277) - returns stale config object → LLMConfig.__init__ (config.py:23-29) - only reads os.getenv once at module load → Frontend loadGlobalAPISettings() - displays config without knowing it's stale

Config Initialization (novel_agent/config.py:20) - load_dotenv() called at module import, only executed once. Web App Startup (novel_agent/web/app.py:47-70) - lifespan() calls config.init() at line 50, no config reload

Critical Write Operations (No Error Handling): novel_agent/web/app.py:325 - save_settings writes .env file (CRITICAL). novel_agent/web/app.py:1226 - save_knowledge_base_config writes JSON (CRITICAL). novel_agent/agent_config.py:279 - save agent configs (HIGH)
